name: Alan Liang
title: Student
description: I am a undergraduate student at the University of Wisconsin-Madison pursuing majors in Computer Science and Enviornmental Studies with a certificate in Data Science. I am excited to learn new skills and apply them to tackle world problems. I'm passionate about fullstack development, AI governance, and environmental justice. Let's connect and explore the different potentials of technology within our society!

projects.title: Astrologic
projects.description: This is a mini puzzle game I made for a final group project about connecting stars to solve puzzles to create a symphony of stars!

projects.title: SpotLight
projects.description: This 2nd place hack for the Empower 2.0 Hackathon SonicBids track aims to help musicians find a place for them to shine.

projects.title: EmptyFridge Plus
projects.description: EmptyFridge Plus allows users to input ingredients they have in their fridge to generate recipes for them using the Spoonacular API. This is the upgraded version of a previous hackathon project

projects.title: Personal Website v1
projects.description: I made this website completely from scratch as a test of the HTML and CSS skills. This site is now deprecated and no longer available

projects.title: Centsible
projects.description: Centsible is your sensible solution to splitting the bill. Rather than scrolling through your Venmo transaction histories, all the information can be stored in Centsible

projects.title: Carcinisation
projects.description: Carcinisation is a resource management game I made in a one week game design sprint. This was my first exposure to game dev. Click on the github repo to play a demo!

projects.title: Beach Ambassador Website
projects.description: This website is accessible and allows all users to learn about the program, how to get involved, and how to stay safe around our waters.

projects.title: Empty Fridge
projects.description: EmptyFridge is an innovative solution to tackle food waste. It allows users to find tasty recipes to use up leftover ingredients. This project was awarded the Best Sustainability Hack award by Avanade.

work.title: Software Development Engineer Intern
responsibilities: Migrated an outdated service to a modern platform using React, TypeScript, and Express
Cut monthly support tickets by 50% by implementing new features to reduce manual workload
Delivered a full-stack, end-to-end project from design to production in an agile environment
Implemented comprehensive unit tests, achieving 95%+ test coverage to ensure code quality and stability
Set up user metrics using AWS Cloudwatch to track adoption and identify bottlenecks

work.title: Peer Mentor - COMP SCI 571: Building User interfaces
responsibilities: Engaged with students in office hours on UI/UX development tools : HTML, CSS, JS, React.js, React Native, Figma.
Guided students to develop user-centric design principles in web and mobile design
Fostered a positive learning environment in a course of 300+ students

work.title: Beach Ambassador
responsibilities: Developed the official Milwaukee Beach Ambassador Website using HTML, Wordpress, and user focused design principles
Interacted with 500+ beachgoers to spread awareness about beach safety along the Milwaukee lakefront
Engaged with the community at citywide events promoting environmental justice and education

work.title: Instructor
responsibilities: Worked with a dynamic team of instructors to support the learning of over 100 students every day
Mastered socratic questioning to guide kids towards a metacognitive understanding of mathematical processes
Actively engaged with the community at local events to spark interest in mathematics
Guided and trained new employees through the process of effectively tutoring

blogs.title: Reflection on computer science culture among students
blogs.excerpt: Personal reflection on my experience in the bay area as an intern.

blogs.title: Bombs and Bikinis: The Story of Nuclear Testing in the Pacific
blogs.excerpt: History paper I wrote for HIST460 at UW-Madison. Was nominated for undergraduate history writing award
